BLite.Server

Preview
🖥️Overview📦Installation🔌Protocol & API🎨Web Studio📡Client SDKs🔗Version Matrix

📦 Installation

BLite.Server ships as a Docker image, a Windows installer, and a Linux binary. Pick the option that fits your environment.

Docker (Recommended)

The fastest way to get started. Pull and run the official image:

docker pull ghcr.io/entgldb/blite-server:latest

docker run -d \
  --name blite \
  -p 2626:2626 \
  -p 2627:2627 \
  -p 2628:2628 \
  -v ./blite-data:/app/data \
  ghcr.io/entgldb/blite-server:latest

Then open the first-run setup wizard in your browser:

https://localhost:2628/setup
📁 Persistent data: Always mount a host volume to /app/data. Without it, all databases and configuration are lost when the container stops.

Docker Compose

services:
  blite:
    image: ghcr.io/entgldb/blite-server:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "2626:2626"   # gRPC
      - "2627:2627"   # REST
      - "2628:2628"   # Studio
    volumes:
      - ./blite-data:/app/data
    environment:
      - Studio__Enabled=true

Windows Installer

The Windows package is an Inno Setup installer that registers BLite.Server as a Windows Service, installs the binary under Program Files\BLite, and adds a firewall exception for ports 2626–2628.

Download from the GitHub releases page:

https://github.com/EntglDb/BLite.Server/releases/latest

After installation the service is started automatically. Open the Studio at https://localhost:2628/setup to create the root user.

🔒 TLS: The installer generates a self-signed certificate on first run. Replace %ProgramData%\BLite\server.pfx with your own certificate to enable trusted HTTPS.

Linux (systemd)

Download the prebuilt binary and set up a systemd unit:

# Download and extract
wget https://github.com/EntglDb/BLite.Server/releases/latest/download/blite-server-linux-x64.tar.gz
tar -xzf blite-server-linux-x64.tar.gz -C /opt/blite

# Create data directory
mkdir -p /var/lib/blite/data

# Copy service unit
cp /opt/blite/blite-server.service /etc/systemd/system/

# Enable and start
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now blite-server

Service Unit (blite-server.service)

[Unit]
Description=BLite Server
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=notify
WorkingDirectory=/opt/blite
ExecStart=/opt/blite/BLite.Server
Restart=always
User=blite
Environment=ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Production
Environment=BLiteServer__DatabasePath=/var/lib/blite/blite.db
Environment=BLiteServer__DatabasesDirectory=/var/lib/blite/data

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Configuration (appsettings.json)

All settings live in appsettings.json next to the binary (or via environment variables):

{
  "BLiteServer": {
    "DatabasePath":      "blite.db",          // system database file path
    "MaxPageSizeBytes":  16384,              // BLiteEngine page size
    "DatabasesDirectory": "data/tenants"    // tenant .db files
  },
  "Studio": {
    "Enabled": true                       // enable Blazor Studio (off by default in Docker)
  },
  "Kestrel": {
    "Endpoints": {
      "Grpc":   { "Url": "https://*:2626", "Protocols": "Http2" },
      "Rest":   { "Url": "https://*:2627", "Protocols": "Http1AndHttp2" },
      "Studio": { "Url": "https://*:2628", "Protocols": "Http1AndHttp2" }
    }
  },
  "Transactions": {
    "TimeoutSeconds": 60
  }
}

Environment Variable Override

Any setting can be overridden via environment variables using __ as the section separator:

# Examples
BLiteServer__DatabasePath=/data/system.db
Studio__Enabled=true
Transactions__TimeoutSeconds=120

First Run

On the very first start, no users exist yet. Navigate to the Studio setup wizard to create the root user:

https://localhost:2628/setup

Create the root admin API key. Store the plaintext key immediately — it cannot be recovered after the setup screen is closed. All subsequent requests require this key (or a key created for a sub-user).

🛡 Dev Mode: Until setup is complete, the server operates in Dev Mode — all requests are authenticated as a synthetic root user. Dev Mode ends permanently once the first real user is saved.
